recipes.title: Goulash
recipes.description: Hungarian beef and paprika soup-stew with potatoes, peppers and caraway.
steps: Cube beef into 3cm pieces.
Sauté finely chopped onions in lard until deep gold.
Off heat, stir in paprika, caraway and crushed garlic so they bloom but do not burn.
Return to low heat, add beef and 200ml water; simmer covered 60 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, peppers, carrots and bay; cover with water and simmer 60 minutes.
Add cubed potatoes and salt; cook 25 minutes until tender.
Adjust seasoning; the consistency should be soupy.

recipes.title: Chicken Paprikash
recipes.description: Hungarian braised chicken in a velvety paprika and sour cream sauce.
steps: Sauté finely chopped onions in lard until soft and golden.
Off heat, stir in paprika to bloom.
Return to low heat, add chicken thighs and brown lightly.
Add chopped pepper, tomato, salt and stock; cover and simmer 35 minutes.
Whisk flour into sour cream; temper with hot sauce, then stir back into pot.
Simmer gently 5 more minutes; do not boil hard.
Serve with nokedli or wide egg noodles.

recipes.title: Lángos
recipes.description: Hungarian fried flatbread topped with garlic, sour cream and grated cheese.
steps: Stir yeast and sugar into warm milk; rest 10 minutes.
Mash potato finely; combine with flour, salt, milk-yeast mix and knead 8 minutes.
Cover and rise 90 minutes until doubled.
Divide into 8 pieces and stretch each thinly with floured hands.
Heat oil to 180°C in a wide pan.
Slip lángos into the oil and fry until puffed and golden, turning once.
Drain briefly, then rub with crushed garlic.
Top with sour cream and grated cheese; serve immediately.

recipes.title: Töltött Káposzta
recipes.description: Hungarian stuffed cabbage rolls of pork and rice baked in paprika-tomato sauerkraut sauce.
steps: Sauté chopped onion in lard until soft; off heat, stir in paprikas.
Combine pork, half-cooked rice, onion-paprika mix, garlic, egg and salt.
Place 2 tablespoons filling on each leaf, fold and roll tightly.
Spread half the sauerkraut in a heavy pot; pack rolls seam-down on top.
Cover with remaining sauerkraut and sliced sausage.
Pour passata and 500ml water over; cover and simmer 90 minutes.
Serve rolls with sauerkraut, sausage and a generous spoon of sour cream.

recipes.title: Halászlé
recipes.description: Fiery Hungarian fisherman's soup of carp and other river fish in a paprika-rich broth.
steps: Fillet fish; reserve heads, bones and trimmings.
Place trimmings, sliced onions and 1.5L water in a wide pot; simmer 60 minutes.
Strain stock and discard solids; return to pot.
Add paprikas, chopped tomato, sliced pepper and cherry peppers; simmer 15 minutes.
Cut fish fillets into thick chunks and salt lightly.
Slip fish into the soup and simmer gently 10 minutes; do not stir.
Adjust seasoning and serve fiery hot with white bread.

recipes.title: Pörkölt
recipes.description: Hungarian thick paprika stew of beef or pork, deep red and onion-rich.
steps: Cube beef into 3cm pieces.
Sauté finely chopped onions in lard until very soft and deep gold.
Off heat, stir in paprika, caraway and crushed garlic.
Add beef, salt, chopped tomato, sliced pepper and bay; do not add water yet.
Cover and simmer on lowest heat 30 minutes until meat releases its juices.
Continue simmering 90 minutes, adding splashes of water only when needed.
Sauce should be thick and clinging; serve with nokedli.

recipes.title: Dobos Torte
recipes.description: Hungarian layered sponge cake with chocolate buttercream and a glassy caramel top.
steps: Whisk egg yolks with half the sugar until pale; whisk whites with rest of sugar to firm peaks.
Fold whites and sifted flour into yolks gently.
Spread thin discs (24cm) on parchment and bake 6 sponges at 200°C for 6 minutes each.
Trim discs to identical circles.
Beat softened butter with icing sugar; whisk in melted chocolate, yolks and vanilla.
Stack sponges with chocolate buttercream between each layer; reserve top sponge.
Cook extra sugar with lemon juice to deep amber caramel.
Pour caramel over the top sponge, score wedges with an oiled knife before it sets.
Place caramel disc on the cake and finish sides with buttercream; chill 2 hours.

recipes.title: Lecsó
recipes.description: Hungarian summer stew of sweet peppers, tomato, onion and paprika, often with sausage or egg.
steps: Slice peppers into rings and tomatoes into chunks.
Sauté chopped onions in lard until soft.
Add sliced sausage and cook 5 minutes until oil is rich red.
Off heat, stir in paprika and crushed garlic.
Return to heat, add peppers, tomatoes and salt.
Simmer uncovered 25 minutes until peppers are tender and sauce thick.
Crack eggs over the top and stir gently until just set.
Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Túrós Csusza
recipes.description: Hungarian pasta with fresh curd cheese, sour cream and crispy bacon.
steps: Dice bacon and render slowly in a pan until very crisp; reserve fat.
Boil noodles in salted water until al dente; drain.
Toss hot noodles with the warm bacon fat.
Crumble túró over the pasta and add sour cream; toss gently.
Season with salt and pepper.
Top with the crispy bacon bits and serve immediately.
